qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated REST API permits complete configuration takeover when insecure mode enabled
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an unauthenticated REST API configuration takeover in pkg/provider/rest/rest.go. The advisory describes HTTP/2 frame handling causing server panic (CWE-476) in golang.org/x/net/http2. These are completely different root causes: one is missing authentication on a REST endpoint, the other is a NULL pointer dereference in HTTP/2 frame processing.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-4hjq-9h5c-252j) describes a NULL pointer dereference (CWE-476) in golang.org/x/net/http2 HTTP/2 frame handling that causes the Traefik server to panic/crash. The sole qualifying finding 'rest_api_config_takeover' addresses missing authentication on the REST provider endpoint (pkg/provider/rest/rest.go), which is a different vulnerable code path and root cause entirely.
evidence: Advisory root cause: HTTP/2 frames cause server panic via NULL pointer dereference in golang.org/x/net/http2 (CWE-476)
Advisory references: https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/net/http2, patched in Traefik v2.11.40 and v3.6.10
Finding 'rest_api_config_takeover' root cause: missing authentication on REST provider ServeHTTP handler in pkg/provider/rest/rest.go:41-60
No finding references HTTP/2 frame handling, golang.org/x/net/http2, or any NULL pointer dereference condition
